---
title: ""
main-photo: Profile.png
mainfont: serif  
fontsize: 10pt
page-layout: full
cv:
  pdf: "Kelly_CV.pdf"
---

```{=html}
<style>
  body {
transform: scale(1.0);
  transform-origin: 0 0;               /* scale from the top-left */
  width: calc(100% / 1.0);   
  
  margin: 0;
  padding: 0;
  }
  .anchorjs-link {
  display: none !important;
</style>

<style>
ul {
  list-style-type: circle; /* options: disc | circle | square | none */
}
</style>
```

```{=css}

.justified {
  text-align: justify;
}

.dash-list li::before {
  content: "– ";             /* En dash (–) or use "-" for hyphen */
  color: #000;               /* You can style the dash (color, size, etc.) */
}
```
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.7.2/css/all.min.css">

:::::::: {style="max-width: 900px; margin: 0 auto;"}
```{=html}
<style>
    a {
      color: #2c7a2c;
      text-decoration: none;
    }

    a:hover {
      text-decoration: underline; 
    }

    }
    
  </style>
```

<br> <br>

::::::: grid
:::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-4}
<font size="5">Edmund Kelly</font>

```{=html}
<div class="grid" style="--bs-columns: 5; row-gap: 0;">
  <div class="g-col-1 g-col-sm-0"></div>
  <div class="g-col-3 g-col-sm-5">
    <picture>
      <source srcset="{{< meta main-photo >}}">
      <img class="circular_image" src="{{< meta main-photo >}}" alt="An image of me.">
      <figcaption><br> </figcaption>
    </picture>
  </div>
  <div class="g-col-1 g-col-sm-0"></div>
</div>
```

::: {style="line-height: 1.5;"}
PhD student<br> Harris Manchester College<br> University of Oxford<br> edmund.kelly\[at\]politics.ox.ac.uk <br> <br> <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=oi6BV7sAAAAJ&hl=en" target="_blank"> <i class="fab fa-google-scholar" style="font-size: 20px;"></i> </a>   <!-- space between icons --> <a href="https://bsky.app/profile/edmundkelly.bsky.social" target="_blank"> <i class="fab fa-bluesky" style="font-size: 20px;"></i> </a>   <!-- space between icons --> <a href="mailto:edmund.kelly@politics.ox.ac.uk" target="_blank"> <i class="fa fa-envelope" style="font-size: 20px;"></i> </a>   <a class="cv-download" href="Kelly_CV.pdf" download="Kelly_CV" target="_blank" style="vertical-align: top;"> CV </a>
:::
::::

:::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-7}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-7 .justified style="text-align: justify;"}
<p>Thank you for visiting! I am a PhD student in the <a href="https://www.politics.ox.ac.uk/person/edmund-kelly">Department of Politics and International Relations</a> at the University of Oxford. I study public opinion and political behaviour in democracies, with a focus on political trust. I do this primarily by analysing quantitative survey data from long-run panels and family/twin studies. I also engage in methodological research about the reproducibility of the social sciences. My work has been published in the <a href="revisiting-the-link-between-political-trust-and-political-participation.pdf" target="_blank">Journal of Politics</a> and the <a href="misconduct-by-voters-own-representatives-does-not-affect-voters-generalized-political-trust.pdf" target="_blank">British Journal of Political Science</a>, and it has been mentioned in <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/commentisfree/2023/dec/05/brexit-leave-remain-tories-labour-voting-trends" target="_blank"> The Guardian</a>.</p>

<h4>Publications</h4>

<ol reversed>

<li><a class="cv-download" href="EducTrust.pdf" target="_blank"> Educational attainment and political trust </a> (with Qinya Feng). American Journal of Political Science (conditionally accepted).</li>

<li><a href="https://www.econstor.eu/bitstream/10419/308508/1/I4R-DP195.pdf?trk=public_post_comment-text"> Comparing human-only, AI-assisted, and AI-led teams on assessing research reproducibility in quantitative social science </a> (with Abel Brodeur, David Valenta, Alexandru Marcoci, et al.). Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences (in press).</li>

<li><a href="revisiting-the-link-between-political-trust-and-political-participation.pdf" target="_blank"> Revisiting the link between political trust and political participation </a> (with James Tilley and Sven Oskarsson). Journal of Politics 88(3).</li>

<li><a href="https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=4790780"> Reproducibility and robustness of economics and political science research </a> (with Abel Brodeur, Derek Mikola, Nikolai Cook, et al.). Nature 652(8108).</li>

<li><a href="misconduct-by-voters-own-representatives-does-not-affect-voters-generalized-political-trust.pdf" target="_blank"> Misconduct by voters' own representatives does not affect voters' generalized political trust </a> (with James Tilley). British Journal of Political Science 54(4).</li>

</ol>

<h4>Working papers</h4>

<ul>

<li><a class="cv-download" href="PersonalityTrust.pdf" target="_blank"> Personality traits and political trust</a>. R&R, British Journal of Political Science</li>

<li><a class="cv-download" href="StrategicProblems.pdf" target="_blank"> Can artificial intelligence solve strategic decision problems?</a> (with Peter Eso, Eszter Kabos, and James Tilley)</a>. Under review. </li>

<li><a class="cv-download" href="DemocracyTrust.pdf" target="_blank"> Political distrust and preferences for regime type</a>.</li>

<li><a class="cv-download" href="PolarizationTrust.pdf" target="_blank"> Mass polarization and political distrust</a>.</li>


</ul>
:::
::::
:::::::
::::::::
